Ha, Mîm.
The bestowal of the Scripture from on high is by Allah the Almighty, the All-Wise.
Indeed, in the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers.
And in yourFootnote-1own creation, and in the treading creatures He scatters, are signs for people who believe with certitude.

And the alternation of night and day, and the provision Allah has sent down from the sky, with which He has revived the earth after its death, and the shifting of the windsFootnote-2 are signs for people who reason.
Those are Allah’s signsFootnote-3which We read to youFootnote-4in truth. So in which discourse, after Allah and His signs, will theyFootnote-5 believe?
Woe to every sinful liar
who hears Allah’s signsFootnote-6being read to him, yet he persists arrogantly, as though he did not hear them. So giveFootnote-7him glad tidingsFootnote-8of a painful punishment.
And when he learns something of Our signs, he takes them in mockery; for thoseFootnote-9is a demeaning punishment.
Beyond them lies Hell, and what they have earned will not benefit them at all, nor will those whom they took as guardians apart from Allah, and for them is a great punishment.
This is guidance, but those who have denied their Lord’s signs—for them is a painful punishment of tormentFootnote-10 .
Allah is the One Who has subjected to youFootnote-11the sea, that ships may run in it by His Command, and that you may seek of His grace, and that you may give thanks.
And He has subjected to youFootnote-12whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth—all is from Him. Indeed, in that are signs for people who reflect.
TellFootnote-13those who have attained faith to forgive those who do not expect the Days of Allah, that He may repayFootnote-14 peoplefor what they used to earn.
Whoever acts righteously does so to his own benefit, while whoever commits evil does so to his own detriment, then to yourFootnote-15Lord you are returnedFootnote-16 .
And surely We had given the Children of Israel the Scripture and wise judgment and prophethood, and We provided them with good things and favored them above all communities

and gave them evident explanations regarding matters. Thus they differed not except after knowledge had come to them, thereby oppressing one another. Indeed, yourFootnote-17Lord judges between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding whatever they used to differ over.
Then We set youFootnote-18upon a(path of) legislation regarding matters, so follow it and do not follow the whims of those who do not know.
Indeed, they will never avail youFootnote-19against Allah in any way, for indeed, the unjust are guardians of one another, while Allah is the Ultimate Guardian of the ones mindful(of Him) .
This provides insights for mankind, and guidance and mercy for people who believe with certitude.

Or do those who have perpetrated evil deeds assume that We will make them asequal to those who have attained faith and have done righteous deeds, whetherFootnote-20 in their life or their death? Miserableis their judgment!
And Allah created the heavens and the earth with truth, and so that every soul will be repaid for what it has earned, for they will not be dealt with unjustly.

Have youFootnote-21seen the one who has taken his own whim as his god, and Allah has misguided him despite his knowledge, and has sealed his hearing and his heart, and has placed a veil over his sight? Who will guide him after Allah? Will you not remindFootnote-22 yourselves?
And they said, “There is nothing but our Earlier Life; we die and we live, for nothing annihilates us except time.” But they have no knowledge of that; they are only guessing.
And when Our signsFootnote-23are recited to them, evident, their only argument is to say, “Bring back our forefathers, if youFootnote-24are truthful.”
SayFootnote-25, “Allah gives you life, then He makes you die, then He gathers you for the Day of Resurrection about which there is no doubt; but most of mankind does not know.”
And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. Thus on the day when the Hour takes place—on that day the advocates of falsehood lose.
And youFootnote-26see every community kneeling; everyFootnote-27 community iscalled to its Scripture: “Today youFootnote-28are being repaid for what you used to do:
here is Our Scripture speaking about youFootnote-29in truth. Surely, We have been transcribing what youFootnote-30have been doing.”
As for those who have attained faith and have done righteous deeds—their Lord enters them into His mercy; that is the evident triumph.
But as for those who have denied(it will be said to them) : “Were My signs not read to youFootnote-31, yet you acted arrogantly and were a criminal people?”

And when it is said, “Indeed, the promise of Allah is true”—while of the HourFootnote-32 there is no doubt—youFootnote-33say, “We do not know what the Hour is; we are only making a guess; we are not certain.”
And the evils of what they had done became evident to themFootnote-34, and they became surrounded by what they used to ridicule.
And it was said, “Today We forget youFootnote-35as you forgot the meeting of this day of yours. Thus your shelter is the Fire, and there are no helpers for you.

That is because youFootnote-36dealt with Allah’s signsFootnote-37with ridicule, and the Earlier Life deceived you.” So today they are not broughtFootnote-38 outof it, and they are not allowed to make amends.
So praise be to Allah—Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of all realms.
And to Him belongs all supremacy in the heavens and the earth, for He is the Almighty, the All-Wise.
